party_affiliation.name: Party affiliation (BR)
party_affiliation.question_text: Generally speaking, do you think of yourself as a member of the…

party_affiliation_probe.name: Party affiliation probe (BR)
party_affiliation_probe.question_text: Do you sympathize with a political party?

party_affiliation_sympathy.name: Party affiliation syampthy (BR)
party_affiliation_sympathy.question_text: Which political party do you sympathize with?

pt_therm_1.name: PT thermometer
pt_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the PT, whose presidential candidate in 2022 was Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva?

pl_therm_1.name: PL thermometer
pl_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the PL, whose presidential candidate in 2022 was Jair Bolsonaro?

mdb_therm_1.name: MDB thermometer
mdb_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the MDB, whose presidential candidate in 2022 was Simone Tebet?

psdb_therm_1.name: PSDB thermometer
psdb_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the PSDB?

psol_therm_1.name: PSOL Feeling Thermometer
psol_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the PSOL?

pdt_therm_1.name: PDT thermometer
pdt_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the PDT, whose presidential candidate in 2022 was Ciro Gomes?

novo_therm_1.name: NOVO thermometer
novo_therm_1.question_text: We'd like you to rate how you feel towards some groups on a scale of 0 to 100. Zero means very unfavorable and 100 means very favorable. Fifty means you do not feel favorable or unfavorable. How would you rate your feeling toward the NOVO, whose presidential candidate in 2022 was Felipe d’Avila?

general_trust.name: General Trust
general_trust.question_text: Do you think your wallet (or your valuables) would be returned to you if it were found by a stranger?

institutional_corruption.name: Institutional Corruption
institutional_corruption.question_text: If a member of the Chamber of Deputies were offered a bribe to influence the awarding of a government contract, do you think that the member of the Chamber of Deputies would accept or refuse the bribe?

options.3: Equally likely to refuse or accept

institutional_response.name: Institutional Response
institutional_response.question_text: If you were to complain about the poor quality of a public service, how likely or unlikely is it that the problem would be easily resolved?

vote_importance.name: Importance of Voting
vote_importance.question_text: How important or unimportant is it to vote in every election?

pride.name: Pride
pride.question_text: How proud are you to be Brazilian?

fair_treatment.name: Fair treatment from government authorities
fair_treatment.question_text: Do you agree or disagree that you can expect fair treatment from government authorities?

democracy_importance.name: Importance of Democracy
democracy_importance.question_text: A representative democracy is a system of government where citizens elect officials who make and enforce laws on their behalf, such as Brazil. How important or unimportant is it to you that you live in a representative democracy?

norm_judges.name: Norm judges
norm_judges.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: {inparty: party of the respondent} elected officials should sometimes consider ignoring supreme court decisions when the judges who issued those decisions were appointed by {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} presidents

norm_judges_perception.name: Norm judges perception
norm_judges_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} elected officials should sometimes consider ignoring supreme court decisions when the judges who issued those decisions were appointed by {inparty: party of the respondent} presidents.

norm_polling.name: Norm polling
norm_polling.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: {inparty: party of the respondent} should reduce the number of polling stations in areas that typically support {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie}.

norm_polling_perception.name: Norm polling perception
norm_polling_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} should reduce the number of polling stations in areas that typically support {inparty: party of the respondent}.

norm_executive.name: Norm executive
norm_executive.question_text: Do you agree or disagree: If a {inparty: party of the respondent} president can't get cooperation from {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} members of congress to pass new laws, the {inparty: party of the respondent} president should circumvent Congress and issue a decree on their own to accomplish their priorities.

norm_executive_perception.name: Norm executive perception
norm_executive_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: If a {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} president can't get cooperation from {inparty: party of the respondent} members of congress to pass new laws, the {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} president should issue decree on their own to accomplish their priorities.

norm_censorship.name: Norm censorship
norm_censorship.question_text: Do you agree or disagree with the following: The government should be able to censor media sources that spend more time attacking {inparty: party of the respondent} than {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie}.

norm_censorship_perception.name: Norm censorship perception
norm_censorship_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following: The government should be able to censor media sources that spend more time attacking {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} than {inparty: party of the respondent}.

norm_loyalty.name: Norm loyalty
norm_loyalty.question_text: Do you agree or disagree with the following:  After an electoral defeat, the {inparty: party of the respondent} candidate should question the result of the elections even when the TSE (Supreme Electoral Tribunal) found no irregularities in the election?

norm_loyalty_perception.name: Norm loyalty perception
norm_loyalty_perception.question_text: What percent of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} voters do you think agree with the following:After an electoral defeat, the {inparty: party of the respondent} candidate should question the result of the elections even when the TSE (Supreme Electoral Tribunal) found no irregularities in the election?

violence1.name: Violence 1
violence1.question_text: Please consider the following scenarios: {name} was convicted of obstructing the right of way during a protest. He was arrested by police after leading a protest against {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} near the STF. He made no effort to not obstruct the right of way  and refused to leave when asked by police. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence2.name: Violence 2
violence2.question_text: {name} was convicted of vandalism. He was arrested by police after he vandalized the election committee of candidates from the {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} party. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence3.name: Violence 3
violence3.question_text: {name} was convicted of assault. He was arrested by police for throwing rocks at peaceful {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} protesters. Although no one was seriously injured, paramedics bandaged a man with a head wound. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence4.name: Violence 4
violence4.question_text: {name} was convicted of arson. He was arrested by police as he attempted to run from a fire he started at the local {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} headquarters. Although he waited for the building to close for the night, several adjacent buildings were still occupied. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence5.name: Violence 5
violence5.question_text: {name} was convicted of assault with a deadly weapon.He was arrested by police after driving his car into a crowd of {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} protesters. Although no one was killed, several individuals were seriously injured and one spent a month in the hospital. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

violence6.name: Violence 6
violence6.question_text: {name} was convicted of murder. He was arrested by police after surveillance footage was found showing him stabbing a prominent {outparty: party with the lowest thermometer score; random draw if there is a tie} to death. {name} targeted the victim because he believed the victim had prevented him from voting in the last election as part of a conspiracy to stop {inparty} voters. Do you support or oppose {name}’s actions?

ethnicity_BR_m_2.name: Pardo (Combined European, Native, and African Ancestry)

income_gross_mo_br.name: Income (BR)
income_gross_mo_br.question_text: Gross family income is the combined income of all individuals who receive any income, including wages, salaries or rents, before tax deductions. What is your gross monthly family income?

GeoPC_Region1_br.name: GeoPC_Region1_br
GeoPC_Region1_br.question_text: Which state do you live in?

Ideo10.name: Ideology (BR) -- Esquerda|Direita
Ideo10.question_text: In general, how would you describe your own political viewpoint?

empl_stat_SCA.name: Employment status
empl_stat_SCA.question_text: Which of the following best describes your current employment status?

options.6: Looking after my home or family

lastvote_br_1.name: Presidential candidate you voted for on October 2, 2022
lastvote_br_1.question_text: In the presidential elections on October 2, 2022, which candidate did you vote for, or perhaps not vote for?

options.6: I invalidated my vote or left it blank
options.7: I didn't vote in the first round

lastvote_br_2.name: Presidential candidate you voted for on October 30, 2022
lastvote_br_2.question_text: In the second round of the presidential elections on October 30, 2022, for which candidate and party did you vote, or perhaps not vote?

options.3: I invalidated my vote or left my vote blank
options.4: I didn't vote in the first round
